_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to connect letters with the sounds they make, so we can read and spell simple three-letter words.

_source.w: We are learning to connect letters with the sounds they make, so we can read and spell simple three-letter words.
y: Knowing the sound each letter makes is the key to cracking the code of reading.
When we can match sounds to letters, we can start writing words by ourselves.
This is the core skill that turns us from beginners into real readers and writers.
eg: We write CVC words like 'mat' by listening to each sound and writing the matching letter.
We read a word like 'pin' by sounding out each letter and blending them together.
We build words with magnetic letters by putting one letter down for each sound.
We play a game where we match picture cards to three-letter words.
We practise reading and writing word families like 'cat, bat, hat, sat'.
We use whiteboards to write a CVC word, then change one letter to make a new word.
